# Claude Managed Agents on Blaxel (self-hosted sandbox cookbook)

> Run Claude Managed Agents (CMA) tool execution on Blaxel sandboxes. Anthropic hosts the agent loop; Blaxel sandboxes run the tools. This self-hosted cookbook uses two sandboxes: an orchestrator sandbox (a FastAPI webhook on a public preview URL) and a worker sandbox (`ant beta:worker poll`). If you are an AI agent working in this repo, read AGENTS.md first.

## The three credentials (keep them straight)

- `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (`sk-ant-api03-…`): standard key, control-plane only (create environment / agent / session). Never on the worker.
- `ANTHROPIC_ENVIRONMENT_KEY` (`sk-ant-oat01-…`): per-environment, scoped + revocable. The worker's work-queue auth, and the only Anthropic secret that enters the worker.
- `ANTHROPIC_WEBHOOK_SIGNING_KEY` (`whsec_…`): verifies inbound webhook signatures on the orchestrator.

## Canonical flow

- Create a self-hosted environment → `ANTHROPIC_ENVIRONMENT_ID`; generate the environment key.
- Build/push the worker image → `sandbox/cma-worker:latest`.
- Create an agent with `agent_toolset_20260401` → `ANTHROPIC_AGENT_ID`.
- A session enqueues work; on `session.status_run_started` Anthropic calls the orchestrator webhook.
- The orchestrator verifies the signature, spawns or reuses one worker per session, and returns 200.
- The worker self-claims from the environment work queue, runs tools in `/workspace`, posts results, and exits on idle; a TTL cleans up the sandbox.

## Key files

- [GUIDE.md](GUIDE.md): full narrative guide — architecture, the 7 setup steps, validated gotchas.
- [README.md](README.md): quickstart.
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): agent onboarding — read first if you are an AI agent.
- [orchestrator/app.py](orchestrator/app.py): webhook verify + per-session spawn/duplicate-suppression + poller launch.
- [worker/Dockerfile](worker/Dockerfile): the agent's runtime (`ant` 1.10.0, node 22, python3, bash, git/curl/tar/unzip).
- [setup.py](setup.py): bring up the orchestrator sandbox and print the webhook URL.
- [example/run_session.py](example/run_session.py): end-to-end example (`--local-worker` skips the webhook).
- [tests/](tests): pytest suite for the orchestrator.

## Commands

- `python3 -m pytest`: orchestrator unit tests (local, safe).
- `python3 example/run_session.py --local-worker`: validate the worker without a webhook (creates a real Anthropic session + Blaxel sandbox).
- `python3 setup.py`: deploy the orchestrator and print the webhook URL.

## Invariants

- File tools use relative paths only; passing an absolute path like `/workspace/hello.txt` is REJECTED with "absolute path not permitted". Shell (bash) commands are unrestricted and use `/workspace/...` paths.
- Every tool call must emit non-empty output (an empty result is rejected with API 400).
- Launch the poller with `keep_alive: True` + a `timeout` cap, or the sandbox standbys mid-session.
- `ttl` is max-age from creation (`m/h/d/w`), not idle, not seconds.

## Links

- Anthropic self-hosted sandboxes: https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/managed-agents/self-hosted-sandboxes
- Blaxel docs: https://docs.blaxel.ai
